title: Master Bundle Atlas - 152 source and handoff packets
public_claim: Master Bundle Atlas is an evidence-governed catalogue for 152 source, handoff, and project packets.
distinctive_mechanism: It routes each packet through artifact identity, evidence state, public claim boundary, portfolio role, and next action so a packet becomes a feature page, source substrate, implementation backlog, private support layer, or archive for stated reasons.
use_value: It matters because a large project archive only becomes professionally legible when readers can see what each packet is, what it supports, and what cannot yet be claimed from it.

flagship_proof_builds.name: 152-packet promotion-router proof
flagship_proof_builds.claim: The Master Bundle Atlas is useful only if it tells which packets become software rooms, research projects, source reservoirs, archive items, or blocked/stub records.
flagship_proof_builds.input: one packet name, manifest entry, text-bearing state, version signal, and intended destination
flagship_proof_builds.operation: classify artifact identity, extract usable substance, assign authority state, and route to portfolio / archive / build backlog
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: packet decision record with promotion route and claim ceiling
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: select one packet and show identity, extraction state, authority status, promotion route, and next build task

component_proofs.component: Master Bundle Atlas - 152 source and handoff packets Proof Boundary
component_proofs.claim_supported: Verified structural coverage: 152 Master Bundle ZIPs observed; 152 per-ZIP expert briefings found; 0 missing briefings; 5,941 archive entries indexed; 4,447 text-bearing entries decoded; 0 extraction failures. The atlas page preserves the 152 briefing records.
component_proofs.input: built assets, partial assets, archive-only assets, and unsupported claims
component_proofs.expected_output: what the packet proves now as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: what the packet proves now as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (built assets, partial assets, archive-only assets...) produces output (what the packet proves now as a reviewable artifact) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.component: Master Bundle Atlas - 152 source and handoff packets Promotion Route
component_proofs.claim_supported: Use the atlas to promote packets responsibly: first classify by evidence state, then route to feature page, source substrate, implementation backlog, private support, or archive; finally bind any stronger public claim to a named live file, route, source.
component_proofs.input: website room, MuranoOS room, Nexus task, paper, source bank, or archive
component_proofs.expected_output: where the packet should go next as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: where the packet should go next as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (website room, MuranoOS room, Nexus task, paper, source...) produces output (where the packet should go next as a reviewable...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.component: Master Bundle Atlas - 152 source and handoff packets Current Authority State
component_proofs.claim_supported: Status: the Master Bundle is structurally audited and navigable in the professional package. It proves an organized 152-packet archive with full briefing coverage, not 152 deployed products.

component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: how strongly the packet may govern a public page

component_proofs.claim_supported: uses `152 Master Bundle ZIPs` to preserve, extract, or route source material before the project raises stronger public claims. Source context: The atlas is grounded in the observed Master Bundle ZIP set and treats each ZIP as a candidate source packet.

minimum_public_demo: select one packet and show identity, extraction state, authority status, promotion route, and next build task
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
